title: Hemizonia increscens
intro: Hemizonia increscens, also known as California tarweed, is a species of flowering plant in the daisy family (Asteraceae) native to the coastal scrub habitats of California and Oregon. Traditionally used in herbal medicine, California tarweed has been reported to exhibit anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may contribute to its potential health benefits, including reducing inflammation and improving respiratory issues. The bioactive constituents of Hemizonia increscens include sesquiterpene lactones, particularly increscensin, as well as other compounds like flavonoids and triterpenoids, which may be responsible for its medicinal properties. In herbal medicine, California tarweed is often prepared as a tincture, infused oil, or dried herb, and is traditionally used to treat various health issues, including respiratory problems and skin conditions.
